en.extract:
The blue-chinned sapphire or blue-chinned emerald (Chlorestes notata) is a hummingbird that ranges from Colombia south and east to the Guianas, Trinidad and Tobago, Peru, and Brazil. There have been occasional records from Tobago. For Brazil, the species' range is along the main Amazon River Basin, as well as the Atlantic Forest, both in the northeast, as well as far south on the southeast coastal strip, (an entire coastal strip, north-east-south of about 3,000 km (1,900 mi)). It is sometimes placed in the genus Chlorostilbon.
It is a bird of forests and sometimes cultivated areas with large trees. The female lays her eggs in a deep cup nest, made of lichen and other fine plant material and placed on a horizontal tree branch.  Incubation is 16 days with a further 18–19 days to fledging.
Blue-chinned sapphires feed on insects and nectar, mainly in trees but sometimes on vines, shrubs, herbs, epiphytes or smaller plants like Heliconia. The song is a high metallic ssooo-ssooo-ssooo.


Description
The blue-chinned sapphire is 8.9 cm (3.5 in) long and weighs 3.8 g (1⁄8 oz). The bill is fairly straight, with the upper mandible black and the lower reddish. The male has mainly green plumage, darker above, with white thighs, a forked metallic blue tail and blue upper throat. The female differs from the male in that she has green-spotted white underparts.


References

ffrench, Richard (1991). A Guide to the Birds of Trinidad and Tobago (2nd ed.). Comstock Publishing. ISBN 0-8014-9792-2.
Hilty, Steven L (2003). Birds of Venezuela. London: Christopher Helm. ISBN 0-7136-6418-5.


External links
Blue-chinned sapphire photo gallery Archived 2016-03-03 at the Wayback Machine VIREO

''Trochilus notatus'' | range_map = Chlorestes notata map.svg }} The '''blue-chinned sapphire''' or '''blue-chinned emerald''' ('''''Chlorestes notata''''') is a hummingbird that ranges from Colombia south and east to the Guianas, [[Trinidad]] and Tobago, Peru, and Brazil.{{Cite book |last=Fogden |first=Michael |title=Hummingbirds: A life sized guide to every species |last2=Taylor |first2=Marianne |last3=Williamson |first3=Sheri L. |publisher=Harper Design |year=2014 |isbn=978-0-06-228064-0 |location=United States, Canada |page=213 |language=English}} There have been occasional records from [[Tobago]]. For Brazil, the species' range is along the main [[Amazon River]] [[Amazon Basin|Basin]], as well as the [[Atlantic Forest]], both in the northeast, as well as far south on the southeast coastal strip, (an entire coastal strip, north-east-south of about {{cvt|3000|km}}). It is sometimes placed in the [[genus]] ''[[Chlorostilbon]]''. It is a [[bird]] of forests and sometimes cultivated areas with large trees. The female lays her eggs in a deep cup nest, made of [[lichen]] and other fine plant material and placed on a horizontal tree branch. Incubation is 16 days with a further 18–19 days to fledging. Blue-chinned sapphires feed on [[insect]]s and nectar, mainly in trees but sometimes on vines, shrubs, herbs, epiphytes{{cite web|url=https://sta.uwi.edu/fst/lifesciences/sites/default/files/lifesciences/documents/ogatt/Chlorostilbon_notatus%20-%20Blue-chinned%20Sapphire.pdf|format=PDF|title=''Chlorostilbon notatus'' (Blue-chinned Sapphire)|website=Sta.uwi.edu|access-date=9 April 2022}} or smaller plants like ''[[Heliconia]]''. The song is a high metallic ''{{not a typo|ssooo-ssooo-ssooo}}''. == Description == The blue-chinned sapphire is {{cvt|8.9|cm}} long and weighs {{cvt|3.8|g|frac=8}}. The bill is fairly straight, with the upper mandible black and the lower reddish. The male has mainly green plumage, darker above, with white thighs, a forked metallic blue tail and blue upper throat. The female differs from the male in that she has green-spotted white underparts. ==References== {{Reflist}} *{{cite book | last = ffrench | first = Richard | title = A Guide to the Birds of Trinidad and Tobago | edition = 2nd | year = 1991 | publisher = Comstock Publishing | isbn = 0-8014-9792-2 }} *{{cite book | last = Hilty | first = Steven L | title = Birds of Venezuela | publisher = Christopher Helm | year = 2003 | location = London | isbn = 0-7136-6418-5 }} ==External links== * [http://vireo.acnatsci.org/search.html?Form=Search&SEARCHBY=Common&KEYWORDS=blue-chinned+sapphire&showwhat=images&AGE=All&SEX=All&ACT=All&Search=Search&VIEW=All&ORIENTATION=All&RESULTS=24 Blue-chinned sapphire photo gallery] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160303180328/http://vireo.acnatsci.org/search.html?Form=Search&SEARCHBY=Common&KEYWORDS=blue-chinned+sapphire&showwhat=images&AGE=All&SEX=All&ACT=All&Search=Search&VIEW=All&ORIENTATION=All&RESULTS=24 |date=2016-03-03 }} VIREO {{Taxonbar|from=Q690521|from2=Q27996612}} [[Category:Chlorestes]] [[Category:Birds of Venezuela]] [[Category:Birds of the Ecuadorian Amazon]] [[Category:Birds of Peruvian Amazonia]] [[Category:Birds of the Guiana Shield]] [[Category:Birds of the Amazon rainforest]] [[Category:Birds of Trinidad and Tobago]] [[Category:Birds of the Atlantic Forest]] [[Category:Hummingbird species of South America]] [[Category:Birds described in 1793|blue-chinned sapphire]] [[Category:Taxa named by Gottfried Christian Reich|blue-chinned sapphire]] [[Category:Birds of Brazil]]

zh.extract:
蓝颏青蜂鸟（学名：Chlorestes notata）是雨燕目蜂鸟科的一种鸟类。
蓝颏青蜂鸟体重约4.2克，翼长约48毫米，嘴峰长约19.4毫米，喙直，宽约1.8毫米，厚约1.9毫米，上黑下红；跗蹠长约4.3毫米，尾长约30毫米。雄性身体主要为绿色，上部色偏深，雌性腹部为白色，并带有绿色斑点。
蓝颏青蜂鸟在其分布区内为留鸟，其栖息在开放的高大树木组成的森林中，食性为草食性，主要食物来源是植物的花蜜。


亚种
C. n. notata：分布于哥伦比亚北部至委内瑞拉、特立尼达岛、多巴哥、圭亚那、巴西东部。
C. n. puruensis：分布于哥伦比亚东南部至秘鲁东北部和巴西西北部。
C. n. obsoleta：分布于秘鲁东北部。
